Off Calendar Feb-20-2018 Off Calendar

Application To Lodge Documents Provisionally Under Seal, Pending Respondents Motion.
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Continued To Mar-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: Prior to the hearing, the Court was notified that the parties in this case have agreed to move the hearing. Therefore, PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Application To Lodge Documents Provisionally Under Seal, Pending Respondent's Motion is continued to March 15, 2018 at 9:30 am in Department 302 pursuant to the parties' stipulation. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena
Off Calendar Jan-31-2018 Continued To Mar-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: Prior to the hearing, the Court was notified that the parties in this case have agreed to move the hearing. Therefore, PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena is continued to March 15, 2018 at 9:30 am in Department 302 pursuant to the parties' stipulation. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Continued To Jan-31-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: Pursuant to the parties' stipulation, the Court continued PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena to January 31, 2018 at 9:30 am. Stipulation and Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Application To Lodge Documents Provisionally Under Seal, Pending Respondents Motion.
Off Calendar Jan-03-2018 Continued To Jan-31-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: Pursuant to the parties' stipulation, the Court continued PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Application To Lodge Documents Provisionally Under Seal, Pending Respondent's Motion to January 31, 2018 at 9:30 am. Stipulation and Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Continued To Jan-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorneys, Kenneth M. Walczak and Austin Yang of the San Francisco City Attorney;s Office (415-554-4206) appear as counsels for petitioner/moving party, City and County of San Francisco

Attorneys, Andrew Cath Rubenstein and Mari Overbeck of the law offices of Munger Tolles & Olson LLP (415-512-4025) appear as counsels for respondent/opposing party, Lyft, Inc.

Court and counsel confer as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Counsel resolve certain issues shall continue to meet and confer. Petitioner City and County of San Francisco may file a motion for relief from "Attorneys' Eyyes Only" designation.

PETITIONER, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena is CONTINUED as a control date only to January 3, 2018 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion, pending motion for relief or further order of the Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Application To Lodge Documents Provisionally Under Seal, Pending Respondents Motion.
Off Calendar Nov-09-2017 Continued To Jan-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorneys, Kenneth M. Walczak and Austin Yang of the San Francisco City Attorney;s Office (415-554-4206) appear as counsels for petitioner/moving party, City and County of San Francisco

Attorneys, Andrew Cath Rubenstein and Mari Overbeck of the law offices of Munger Tolles & Olson LLP (415-512-4025) appear as counsels for respondent/opposing party, Lyft, Inc.

Court and counsel confer as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Counsel resolve certain issues shall continue to meet and confer. Petitioner City and County of San Francisco may file a motion for relief from "Attorneys' Eyyes Only" designation.

PETITIONER, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Application To Lodge Documents Provisionally Under Seal, Pending Respondents Motion is CONTINUED as a control date only to January 3, 2018 at 9:30 AM in Department 302/Law and Motion, pending motion for relief or further order of the Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR No. 6576, e-mail: rhunter@sftc.org. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Continued To Nov-09-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorneys, Kenneth M. Walczak and Austin Yang of the San Francisco City Attorney;s Office (415-554-4206) appear as counsels for petitioner/moving party, City and County of San Francisco

Attorneys, Jonathan H. Blavin and Mari Overbeck of the law offices of Munger Tolles & Olson LLP (415-512-4011) appear as counsels for respondent/opposing party, Lyft, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

A request for relief from "Attorney Eyes Only" designation is DENIED. Court makes interim orders regarding production. Defendant, Lyft need not produce anything further. Otherwise, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas is Continued for further hearing on November 9, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302. Further notice is waived. Counsel for defendant, Lyft shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR No. 9711, e-mail: lreding@sftc.org. (L&M302)

PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena....is in progress

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoena
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Continued To Oct-05-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kenneth M. Walczak, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.4206, for petitioner CCSF;
Mari Overbeck, Esq. and Jonathan Blavin, Esq. of Munger, Tolles & Olson LLP, Tel. 415.512.4064, for respondent LYFT, Inc.

PETITIONER CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Enforce Administrative Subpoenas was discussed. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Petitioner City and County of San Francisco's motion to enforce administrative subpoenas is continued to October 5, 2017 to give the parties an opportunity to engage in further meet and confer efforts to narrow or possibly eliminate the issues in dispute. In addition, the Court granted petitioner's oral request to file supplemental briefs. Petitioner CCSF may file its brief on 10/02/17; respondents may file a response no later than 10/04/17. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.
